mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production index directly proxies real output expansion, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following positive INDPRO changes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month unemployment rate increase greater than 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating household income expectations and falling consumption, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when UNRATE rises by more than 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in CPIAUCSL combined with month-over-month declines in INDPRO signal stagflationary margin compression, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL rises and INDPRO falls.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, month-over-month declines in retail sales signal that higher policy rates are successfully choking off consumer demand, amplifying negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following RSAFS declines are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0% (Rank IC > 0.0 over a 3-year window).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate from the prior month signals tighter short-term liquidity, driving negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts level below 1,000 thousand units signals insufficient residential investment and weak construction employment, producing negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following HOUST readings below 1000 are not negative (mean return > 0) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth signals expanding household income and consumption demand, driving positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS changes do not coincide with positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 52% of months over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth over a 3-month window signals capacity utilization improvements and operating leverage gains, producing positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following 3-month INDPRO growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 4-year rolling window.
